OPPOSITION MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES RE MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF DANIELS, MYRA

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. AT THIS STAGE OF THE CASE, THE COMPLAINT IS NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS OR PRECLUDED BY THE PRIOR SETTLEMENTS. THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE THAT AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENTS BAR CLAIMS ARISING FROM BACK-UPS OCCURRING AFTER JULY 9, 2008. THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS DOES NOT NECESSARILY AND CLEARLY BAR EVERY CLAIM THE FIRST, THIRD, AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT UNCERTAIN. THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT UNCERTAIN OR BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AND PLAINTIFF HAS STANDING TO PURSUE THAT CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AS TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AND DENIED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS ARE INSUFFICIENT, BUT PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE, IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS SHOWING THAT THE DEFENDANT ACTED WITH MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION. A MOTION TO STRIKE IS NOT THE PROPER PROCEDURE TO CHALLENGE THE ENTIRETY OF A CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Helen Lee Greenberg, Esq., of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP, (415-438-6689) for All Hallows Preservation LP, A California Limited Partnership, Defendant and moving party.
Frank B. Falkenburg, Esq., of Murray & Asociates, (415-673-0555),for Myra Daniels, Plaintiff and opposing party.
Court Reporter Alice Chang, CSR#13654 (415-591-3333)

ARGUED. Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift burden. As to waiver and res judicata/collateral estoppel grounds, Defendant failed to show that all of Plaintiff's claims arise from incidents concerning backed-up sewage, which occurred anytime from April of 2004 through July 9, 2008 (date the Release was signed). The Court does not need to reach the issue of enforceability of the Release as none of the causes of action can be completely resolved at this point.
As to the Statute of Limitations ground, Defendant's evidence does not support UMF 26 and does not satisfy Miller requirements for accrual of cause of action, i.e. (1) appreciable harm; and (2) awareness that defendant's negligence caused harm. Specifically, SAC 17-19 and Daniels Depo., Volume 1 at 21:16-21 does not satisfy "awareness" requirement. (Miller v. Lakeside Village Condominium Assn. (1991) 1 Cal.App.4th 1611.

Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Defendant failed to shift burden and establish that Defendant is entitled to summary adjudication as a matter of law due to plaintiff's failure to provide evidence to support punitive damages claim. Unlike in College Hospital, Plaintiff here alleged and provided evidence that defendant knew or possessed information regarding multiple floods, plaintiff's health issues and did not address the cause of the floods. Therefore, it is an issue of fact to be determined by the trier whether defendant's disregard of Plaintiff's rights for so contemptible that it would be looked down upon and despised by reasonable people.

Orders signed in open court. Judge: Harry M. Dorfman, Clerk: Mars Goodman, Court Reporter Alice Chang, CSR#13654, (D501)

Mtn To Strike; Req For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Apr-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rowena P. Lizin, Esq., of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP, Tel. 415.438.6617, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Jessica L. Chylik, Esq., of the Law Offices of Jessic L. Chylik, Tel. 415.863.5718, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP'S Motion To Strike Portions of the Complaint is Granted as to the punitive damages allegations with ten days leave to amend and denied as to the third cause of action. The punitive damages allegations are insufficient, but plaintiff is granted leave, if she can do so in good faith, to allege ultimate facts showing that the defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. A motion to strike is not the proper procedure to challenge the entirety of a claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rowena P. Lizin, Esq., of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP, Tel. 415.438.6617, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Jessica L. Chylik, Esq., of the Law Offices of Jessic L. Chylik, Tel. 415.863.5718, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled in its entirety. At this stage of the case, the complaint is not barred by the statute of limitations or precluded by the prior settlements. The court cannot determine that as a matter of law that the settlement agreements bar claims arising from back-ups occurring after July 9, 2008. The statute of limitations does not necessarily and clearly bar every claim The first, third, and fifth causes of action are not uncertain. The ninth cause of action is not uncertain or barred by the statute of limitations and plaintiff has standing to pursue that claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
